title: Pitch Deck Outline
note: A slide-by-slide outline for the investor deck, pulling from across the repository. Rebuilt around the three-phase spine: free app (acquisition) → premium subscription (monetization) → smart hardware (upgrade layer). Built on the wager stated throughout: rigorous honesty is more fundable than polished optimism, for the right investor. Each slide names its source file and the ONE thing it must accomplish.

deck_philosophy.principle: Lead with who's underserved at every stage, sell the phased economics as land-and-expand, volunteer the risks at every phase, and never overclaim a conversion rate, a hardware capability, or a user's trust before it's earned.
deck_philosophy.the_arc: Aquaculture just became the majority of the world's aquatic animal production, and almost none of it is digitally managed at any price point → selling expensive, unproven hardware to that market first means paying full acquisition cost for every customer with no funnel underneath it, which is a go-to-market problem, not a technology problem → the fix is to meet farmers where they are: a free app that any beginner can use today, which is also the cheapest possible way to build trust and real farm data → as a farm grows past manual tracking, a premium tier turns that logged history into revenue → the operations that outgrow the software entirely are the ones who buy the hardware, so it sells as an upgrade into a customer we already know, not a cold sale to a stranger → here is the one thing we must prove at each phase and how we'll prove it cheaply, starting with the cheapest.
what_this_deck_refuses_to_do: Claim a free-to-paid conversion rate before Phase 1 usage data exists
Claim we prevent fish kills before Phase 3 validation
Hide the BVLOS dependency inside the Phase 3 story
Invent app downloads, active users, subscribers, or any other traction
Imply TRL beyond 3 for the hardware layer
Treat the free tier as bait we're impatient with — it is a real product for a real audience, evaluated on its own honest terms

slides.title: Title
slides.must_accomplish: Name, the mark, one line: 'We meet fish farmers where they are — from their first pond to their first drone.'
slides.visual: The logo mark — it encodes aerial-view + seeing-beneath-water in one glyph. Use it.

slides.title: The Inflection
slides.must_accomplish: In 2022 aquaculture passed capture fisheries for the first time — 51% of aquatic animal production. The centre of the industry is now a farmed, instrumentable system, and almost none of it is instrumented, tracked, or even digitally recorded at any price point. Say it once, let it land.

slides.title: The Problem, and Why Hardware-First Doesn't Solve It
slides.must_accomplish: Every farmer is underserved, differently by stage: the beginner gets no guidance and keeps bad paper records, the growing farm guesses because it has outgrown a notebook, the commercial operator can't see the 3D chemical environment that kills stock overnight. Leading with a USD 20k+ drone answers only the last of those, and only for a customer who already trusts you — backwards for a company with no customers yet.

slides.title: The Platform — Three Phases
slides.must_accomplish: The spine, shown once, plainly: Phase 1 free app (acquisition) → Phase 2 premium (monetization) → Phase 3 smart hardware (upgrade layer, moat). This is the slide that must land before any detail — everything after it is that spine unpacked.
slides.visual: A simple three-box funnel: free app widening the top, premium narrowing but monetizing the middle, hardware deepening revenue at the bottom.

slides.title: Phase 1 — Foundation (Free App)
slides.must_accomplish: AI farming assistant, guided farm setup, photo-based disease identification, manual water-quality logging, feeding schedules, community, learning modules. Free or near-free on purpose — the acquisition and data-accumulation engine, and the same product that resolves the old affordability paradox: smallholders are the top of the funnel now, not a deferred segment.

slides.title: Phase 2 — Monetization (Premium)
slides.must_accomplish: Premium turns Phase 1's logged history into revenue: predictive health monitoring trained on the farmer's own data, smart recommendations, a marketplace for feed/equipment/buyers, paid expert consultations. This is where AquaVision becomes a subscription-plus-take-rate business, not a free utility with a hope attached.

slides.title: Phase 3 — Smart Aquaculture (Hardware Upgrade)
slides.must_accomplish: For farms that have outgrown manual and even premium-software tracking: IoT water probes, the OmniDrone, the PondTwin digital twin, an enterprise dashboard. Show the render. This is a hardware sale into a customer the platform already knows, not a cold sale to a stranger.
slides.visual: The product render + the four functions.

slides.title: The Insight That Makes It Defensible
slides.must_accomplish: Two insights, stacked. Engineering: the probe and the camera are one instrument — the drone carries its own ground truth and calibrates its own imagery in a single sortie, which nobody else does. Business: by the time a farm needs that hardware, we already hold years of its own water and production history from Phases 1-2, so the digital twin starts calibrated, not cold. The data moat is built before the hardware ships.

slides.title: A Day in a Farmer's Life
slides.must_accomplish: Follow one farmer across the funnel: logging feedings by phone photo as a free-tier beginner, upgrading to Premium once the farm outgrows notebook tracking and wants predictive alerts, adopting the OmniDrone and IoT sensors once she's running an estate no one can walk in a day. Note it's a storyboard, not a testimonial — none of this has happened yet.

slides.title: How We Sell — The Checkable Economics At Every Phase
slides.must_accomplish: Phase 1 costs the farmer nothing, so there's no ROI pitch — only an activation one. Phase 2 sells against verifiable savings (feed cost via FCR, time saved over manual records). Phase 3 leads with aeration-electricity savings: metered, budgeted, verifiable in one cycle without believing our modelling. At no phase do we lead with the least verifiable claim.

slides.title: Market & Beachhead
slides.must_accomplish: Two markets, stacked: the software aquaculture-management market (Phase 1/2, sized against smallholder and farm-operator counts) and the hardware/monitoring market (Phase 3 — 70% of production in Asia, 62.6% inland, our exact environment). Show the sizing method for both, and name the data still missing for each.
slides.caveat: Do NOT show a fabricated TAM for either layer. Show the method and the missing input for both — it's more credible.

slides.title: Competition — Honestly
slides.must_accomplish: Two competitive sets now, not one. Farm-management and record-keeping apps: we win on aquaculture-specific AI and an upgrade path they don't have, we lose on brand recognition and multi-crop breadth. Drone/monitoring hardware vendors: same honest matrix as before — we win on depth+space+imaging, lose on continuity+weather+cost, and the hybrid with a fixed buoy is our own roadmap. Volunteering both wins trust.

slides.title: Where We Actually Are
slides.must_accomplish: Two honest zeros, not one: the app has zero users and nothing built, the hardware is TRL 3 with nothing flown. Here is the evidence-gated path up for each, with kill criteria. Owning both is the credibility move of the whole deck.

slides.title: The Risks We're Not Hiding
slides.must_accomplish: Phase 1/2 risks: unproven free-to-paid conversion, no track record to trust, 'just another farm app' competition, community-led growth that may not scale. Phase 3 risks: BVLOS dependency, weather grounding, unproven unit economics. Each with a mitigation. This slide is the deck's spine.

slides.title: The Ask
slides.must_accomplish: Fund Phase 1 — building and launching the free app is the cheapest way to de-risk the whole thesis, because a software funnel is testable in months at a fraction of hardware's cost, and it can honestly trigger a stop before a single drone is built. Phase 3 R&D continues in parallel at low burn. Milestone-gated tranches tied to activation and conversion metrics, not a revenue forecast.

slides.title: Why This Team, Why Now
slides.must_accomplish: Real founder credentials (must be written by the founders, never invented) + the software and hardware timing converging. Then the mission: every farmer given the same quality of information, not just the ones who can afford a drone.
slides.caveat: The team slide MUST use real, verifiable biographies. Fabricated credentials end raises.

the_appendix_slides.note: Held in reserve for the questions you know are coming.

slides.for: the 'how does a free app actually make money' question

slides.topic: Why one phased company, not a software spin-off and a separate hardware startup

slides.for: the 'does it make money' question, for each phase

the_two_versions.the_5_minute_version: Slides 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 10, 13, 15. The inflection, the three-stage problem, the phased spine, Phase 1 acquisition, Phase 3 hardware, the checkable economics, the honest zeros, the ask.
the_two_versions.the_owner_version: For the absentee farm owner sizing the Phase 3 hardware (see customer-personas.json): ONE slide — payback period on aeration savings. Different artefact entirely; no Phase 1/2 story, no water chemistry.
the_two_versions.the_impact_version: Add the SDG alignment WITH its tensions, the free-tier-as-equity argument (smallholders are Phase 1's core audience now, not a deferred segment), the occupational-safety argument for Phase 3, and the equity commitment. See sdg-alignment.json.
